serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 400 g (one full cup), as typically served in Singaporean bubble tea and dessert drink outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component ingredients: mango, coconut milk, sago, spirulina, jasmine tea, cane sugar — using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Tables
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Tables for South-East Asia
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for this branded multi-component drink exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis: approximately 35% mango pulp, 30% coconut milk (canned, diluted), 10% sago pearls (cooked), 5% spirulina powder (trace), 15% jasmine tea (brewed), 5% cane sugar syrup. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used for mango (FDC ID 169910), coconut milk (FDC ID 170172), sago (FDC ID 168880), spirulina (FDC ID 169679), and sugar. FAO/INFOODS SEA used for regional calibration. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mango and coconut milk-based beverages — analogous estimate based on mango (GI 51) and coconut milk sago drink components; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific branded drink. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on: mango GI ~51 (University of Sydney GI Database), coconut milk low GI contribution (~35), sago pearls GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database), cane sugar GI ~65, jasmine tea GI ~1. Weighted blend of components yields an estimated composite GI of approximately 55. Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (55 × 43.2 g carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 23.8. Carbohydrate per serving = 10.8 g/100g × 400 g = 43.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~18 mg/100 g); potassium from mango may support blood pressure regulation
hypertension.guidance: With approximately 72 mg sodium per 400 g serving, this drink poses minimal sodium burden for hypertensive individuals, though the added cane sugar content should be monitored as part of overall dietary pattern.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~34 g per serving) and low glycemic index (55) with a high glycemic load of approximately 23.8 per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this drink due to its high sugar load (~34 g per 400 g serving) and a glycemic load of ~23.8, despite its low GI of approximately 55; selecting the no-sugar-added option and reducing portion size are strongly recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk (~11.2 g per serving) may raise LDL cholesterol; high sugar content contributes to triglyceride risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The coconut milk base contributes approximately 11.2 g of saturated fat per 400 g serving, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels in individuals with cardiovascular disease; limiting portion size and frequency of consumption is advised.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Coconut Mango Blue Drink is a chilled, multi-component dessert beverage sold by HeyTea at Marina Bay Sands, Singapore. It is composed of fresh mango pulp, coconut milk, coconut jelly, cooked sago pearls, a small quantity of spirulina (which imparts the characteristic blue-green hue), and a jasmine tea base, sweetened with real cane sugar. The drink has a creamy, tropical flavour profile with a sweet-tart mango foreground, a rich coconut milk body, and chewy textural elements from the sago pearls and coconut jelly. The spirulina contributes a subtle earthy note and a visually striking blue-green colour. Per 100 g, the drink provides approximately 72 kcal, 0.8 g protein, 3.2 g fat (predominantly saturated from coconut milk), and 10.8 g carbohydrate with 8.5 g sugar. A standard 400 g serving delivers approximately 288 kcal and 34 g sugar. The glycemic index is estimated at 55 (low) with a glycemic load of approximately 23.8 per serving, making it a high-concern item for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes due to the overall sugar content and GL. The coconut milk base contributes meaningful saturated fat (~11.2 g per serving), which is relevant for cardiovascular risk management. The product description notes it contains dairy and is sweetened with cane sugar by default; a no-sugar-added option may be requested. This drink is popular in Singapore's bubble tea and dessert beverage culture, reflecting the influence of Chinese-origin tea chains offering fruit-forward, visually distinctive beverages.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for this branded multi-component drink exists in any approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analysis using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA. GI is an analogous estimate based on component GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to estimation methodology.
notes: Nutrient values are estimated from component ingredients (mango, coconut milk, coconut jelly, sago, spirulina, jasmine tea, cane sugar) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Tables. No exact branded product entry exists in any approved primary source. trans_fat_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero — coconut milk and mango contain no industrially produced trans fats; naturally occurring trans fats are negligible and below detection threshold). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero — all primary ingredients are plant-based; dairy noted in vendor description is unspecified and assumed trace or incidental). vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source databases for these plant-based ingredients and is not expected to be present in meaningful quantities. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source databases for these ingredients. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source databases for these ingredients. GI is estimated at 55 (low) based on weighted component GI values from the University of Sydney GI Database; this is an analogous estimate and not a direct measurement. The vendor description states the product contains dairy; the nature of the dairy component is unspecified and may relate to a milk-based topping or modifier not included in the standard recipe. Sugar-free sweetener option was noted as out of stock at time of data collection; cane sugar is the default sweetener. Spirulina provides the characteristic blue-green colour. This record was generated from field survey data seed data (GD000549) and has been processed under Project Veritas v2.

Updated related notes and health context guidance to reflect this change while maintaining the high concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to high sugar and glycemic load.
